life-better-today.context: In your English class you have been talking about life in the past. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
life-better-today.question: 'Life is better today than it was 100 years ago.' Do you agree?

work-money-enjoyment.context: In your English class you have been talking about work. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
work-money-enjoyment.question: Is it better to earn a lot of money or to enjoy your job?
writingPoints: how much time is spent at work
the type of work which is done
job satisfaction and mental health (your own idea)

endangered-animals.context: In your English class you have been talking about animals and the environment. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
endangered-animals.question: 'We should do everything we can to save animals which are in danger of disappearing from our planet.' Do you agree?
writingPoints: the kind of animals which are in danger
the reasons for protecting these animals

friends-vs-family.context: In your English class you have been talking about relationships. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
friends-vs-family.question: Which is more important – friends or family?
writingPoints: who will help you when you have problems

notes.text: main advantage: enjoyment and shared interests
choose friends who share hobbies, values
friends may not «help» in serious problems
different relationships matter at different stages

environment-pollution.context: In your English class you have been talking about the environment. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
environment-pollution.question: Do you think the world's pollution problems can be solved?

technology-social-interaction.context: In your English class you have been talking about technology. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
technology-social-interaction.question: Is technology making us less sociable?

notes.text: main advantage: connects instantly across distances
people «stare» at screens instead of talking

gap-year.context: In your English class you have been talking about what students do after finishing school. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
gap-year.question: Is taking a gap year before university a good idea?

online-shopping.context: In your English class you have been talking about shopping. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
online-shopping.question: Is online shopping better than shopping in traditional stores?
writingPoints: trying products before buying (your own idea)

notes.text: browse and purchase anytime from home
busy people order late at night
many high street stores «forced» to close
«combining» online convenience with local support ideal

childhood-obesity.context: In your English class you have been talking about health. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
childhood-obesity.question: What can be done to reduce childhood obesity?

notes.text: excessive sugary snacks and fast food
many eat crisps and burgers regularly

social-media-addiction.context: In your English class you have been talking about social media. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
social-media-addiction.question: What are the causes and effects of social media addiction?

family-size.context: In your English class you have been talking about families. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
family-size.question: Is it better to be part of a large family or a small family?

notes.text: share limited resources among more children
parents have more time for each child
more help with homework and problems
focus on emotional and educational needs
both sizes have benefits and challenges

work-from-home.context: In your English class you have been talking about work. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
work-from-home.question: Do you agree that working from home is better than working in an office?

notes.text: on other hand: reduces social interaction
benefits «outweigh» drawbacks when social needs addressed

fashion-industry.context: In your English class you have been talking about fashion. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
fashion-industry.question: Does the fashion industry have a bad effect on people's lives?

notes.text: millions of tonnes in landfills annually

learning-languages.context: In your English class you have been talking about language learning. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
learning-languages.question: Are there more reasons to learn a foreign language than just passing a test?

notes.text: YES - many valuable reasons throughout life
leads to better jobs and higher salaries

learning-history.context: In your English class you have been talking about education. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
learning-history.question: Should everyone be taught the history of their own country?

notes.text: YES - everyone should «study» own country's history
«learning» about wars teaches importance of peace

sports-money.context: In your English class you have been talking about sport. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
sports-money.question: Are famous sports people paid too much money?

notes.text: train intensively for years, sacrifice social lives
some «earn» more from endorsements than sport

self-employment.context: In your English class you have been talking about careers. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
self-employment.question: Is it better to be self-employed or to work for somebody else?

notes.text: key disadvantage of self-employment: unpredictable income
self-employed struggle to separate work from leisure
both options have merits and challenges
best choice «depends» on priorities and circumstances

advertising-society.context: In your English class you have been talking about media. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
advertising-society.question: Is advertising a very good thing for society?

helping-others.context: In your English class you have been talking about community. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
helping-others.question: Should everyone regularly do something to help other people?

notes.text: YES - should be important part of life
communities «function» better when people support each other

online-learning.context: In your English class you have been talking about education. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
online-learning.question: Is online learning better than traditional classroom learning?

notes.text: both methods have advantages and challenges
«combining» flexibility with classroom interaction most effective
